expectations.evidence: All FreeRTOS objects use static creation variants (xMessageBufferCreateStatic, xTaskCreateStatic, xQueueCreateStatic). No malloc, new, or std::vector anywhere in the code.
All buffers are declared as static arrays with known sizes: ucMessageBufStorage, usDmaBuffer, uxAdcTaskStack, sPool, ucFreeQStorage, ucReadyQStorage, usDmaBuf.
Uses FreeRTOS ISR-safe APIs: xMessageBufferSendFromISR, xQueueReceiveFromISR, xQueueSendFromISR, with portYIELD_FROM_ISR for proper context switching.
DMA buffers (usDmaBuffer, usDmaBuf) are plain static uint16_t arrays with no explicit alignment. No over-specification to 32-byte alignment. Appropriate for STM32F4 (Cortex-M4, no D-cache).

No custom ring buffer with power-of-2 bitmask indexing is implemented. The solution relies on FreeRTOS Message Buffers and Queues, which internally do not use bitmask indexing. MSG_BUF_SIZE is 289 (not a power of 2), and no bitmask operation (& mask) is used for index wrapping.
